findings.diff_summary: Phase 2 provenance filter steps 4-5 expanded from 2 lines to 8 lines. New content adds BULK-mode-aware staging behavior (BULK=false removes anomalous paths from candidate set; BULK=true is enrichment-only) with 4 distinct warning message variants. This is a functional behavioral change specifying different handling per mode, not decorative prose.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Changes are substantive procedural rules distinguishing BULK=false vs BULK=true behavior, consistent with 'rules not stories' principle.

findings.diff_summary: Changes are purely parameter/behavioral updates: python to python3, CLAUDE_SESSION_ID to CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback, 10 min to 30 min expiry window. No new sections added, no narrative prose introduced.

Push-analyst grant TTL section changed from 11 lines (verbose narrative with multi-sentence rationale) to 8 lines. This is a net verbosity REDUCTION. However, the new body still retains change-history narrative: 'raised from 120s to 180s in task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4, then raised from 180s to 600s in task dev-20260527-063758-T3 to cover the full orchestrator → push-analyst → orchestrator result-processing → execute-push.py round trip, which frequently exceeded the previous 180s window.' The SESSION_ID variable fallback and error message wording are purely behavioral updates.
findings.recommendation: ADVISORY only (sub-threshold: 0 net new verbose lines; the diff reduced verbosity). The TTL body still contains change-history narrative that could be condensed. Per 'rules not stories': consider tightening to 'Default Chain-B grant TTL: 600s (10 min), covering push-analyst dispatch, orchestrator result handling, and execute-push.py. This is separate from the 30-min commit-grant TTL.' Pre-existing baseline context makes this non-blocking.

findings.diff_summary: Phase 7 TTL comment changed from 2 lines to 5 lines (adds 'The TTL is 600s to cover the orchestrator -> push-analyst -> orchestrator result-processing -> execute-push.py round trip, which frequently exceeded the previous 180s window (task dev-20260527-063758-T3; raised from 120s to 180s in task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4, then to 600s here).'). Constant value changed from 180 to 600. Added lines: ~3 rationale lines on 205-line file = 1.5% verbosity delta.
findings.recommendation: ADVISORY only (below 50-line minor threshold). The inline change-history rationale ('frequently exceeded... task dev-20260527-063758-T3; raised from 120s to 180s in task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4, then to 600s here') is borderline explanatory narrative. The task-id citations provide useful traceability. Verbosity delta is 1.5%, well below the 50-line minor threshold. Not blocking.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed no critical/major/minor threshold violations introduced in this diff. Raised two issues with PROPOSED_FIX: (1) scope accounting should explicitly note INDEX.md files skipped (auto-generated, timestamp-only diff); (2) commands/push.md merits an advisory consistent with agents/push-analyst.md advisory since it retains some change-history narrative despite net reduction. Two OBSERVATION_ONLY items noted: line count estimates in draft were slightly off (does not affect severity classification), and the overall conclusion 'no reportable verbosity violations' was confirmed correct.

findings.issue: Scope accounting omitted changed agents/INDEX.md and commands/INDEX.md; they are under agents/commands/ but are auto-generated index files with timestamp-only diffs and should be explicitly skipped rather than absent from the file list.
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit skip note: 'Skipped auto-generated index docs: agents/INDEX.md (+1/-1 timestamp only), commands/INDEX.md (+1/-1 timestamp only). No prompt-instruction prose changed; no verbosity inspection finding.'
findings.rationale: Scope exclusion rationale section added to report to document all excluded files explicitly.

findings.issue: commands/push.md has the same sub-threshold change-history/rationale pattern called out for agents/push-analyst.md. Net reduction is true, but the new TTL body still includes historical narrative. Not reportable minor+, but missed as an advisory.
findings.proposed_fix: Either add an advisory for commands/push.md, or tighten the section to: 'Default Chain-B grant TTL: 600s (10 min), covering push-analyst dispatch, orchestrator result handling, and execute-push.py. This is separate from the 30-min commit-grant TTL.'
findings.rationale: Advisory finding added for commands/push.md to match treatment of agents/push-analyst.md. The fix suggestion (tightened prose) is logged as the recommendation. introduced_in_diff set to false because the TTL section is pre-existing; this diff reduced it, making any remaining narrative a pre-existing issue.

findings.issue: Some draft line counts are inaccurate but do not change severity: agents/changelog-analyst.md changed 2 lines to 8, not 12; agents/push-analyst.md added 4 rationale lines plus one constant change, not '3 added lines'; commands/push.md TTL body is currently 8 lines, not 6.
findings.rationale: Counts corrected in final report. No severity impact — all values remain well below minor (50-line) threshold.

findings.issue: No missed critical/major/minor finding found. The conclusion 'no reportable verbosity violations introduced in this diff' remains correct after the scope/accounting fixes.
findings.rationale: Confirmed no threshold violations. Report conclusion stands.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied Issue 1 (scope exclusion rationale section added for INDEX.md files) and Issue 2 (advisory added for commands/push.md). Corrected line count estimates in diff_summary fields per Issue 3. Issue 4 confirms overall conclusion unchanged.
